actions.summary: Added 301 redirects in .htaccess for all old-site URLs
actions.detail: Configured server-side 301 redirects for /post/*, /netsuite-*, /services/*, /blog/*, /about-erp*, /case-studies-*, and other legacy paths. Google should de-index old URLs and transfer link equity over the next few weeks.
actions.monitor: Check Google Search Console weekly — old URLs should gradually disappear from the index. If 404 exits drop to 0 in the next data update, this issue is resolved.

actions.summary: Added Calendly 'Book a Call' option alongside the contact form
actions.detail: Contact page now offers three paths: a form (6 fields, 3 required), a Calendly booking link, and a NETscore quiz link. This reduces friction for visitors who don't want to fill out a form.
actions.monitor: Track which of the three options gets the most clicks. If drop-off persists after 4+ weeks, the issue is likely intent (visitors researching, not ready to commit) rather than friction.

actions.summary: Added multiple conversion paths (form, Calendly, NETscore quiz)
actions.detail: Every main page now has 'Start a conversation', 'Book a Call', and 'Get your free NETscore' CTAs. Resources and What is NetSuite pages also have a lead magnet guide download.
actions.monitor: Monitor NETscore quiz completions and guide downloads as softer conversion signals. The CTA-to-lead ratio should improve as these alternatives capture visitors who aren't ready for direct contact.

actions.summary: Added CTAs and lead magnets to every main page
actions.detail: Every page now has multiple CTAs including softer options (NETscore quiz, guide download) for visitors who are researching rather than ready to buy.
actions.monitor: Track NETscore quiz and guide download uptake. The browse-to-contact ratio may remain low — most visitors are in research mode. Softer CTAs capture this audience differently.

funnel.description: Active users who visited the site

roadmapPhases.label: Phase 1
roadmapPhases.title: Quick Wins
roadmapPhases.subtitle: Already ranking well — rewrites can drive clicks within weeks

items.title: 6 Essential Accounts Receivable Reports
items.why: Closest to page 1, already getting clicks. A strong rewrite could break top 10 fast. Niche topic = less competition.

items.title: CSV Imports: Why They Fail and How to Fix It
items.why: Already on page 1. Technical credibility piece that shows E3 knows NetSuite inside out.

items.title: 10 Tips to Maximise Profits
items.why: Just off page 1. A light refresh can still push it over.

roadmapPhases.label: Phase 2
roadmapPhases.title: High-Volume Content
roadmapPhases.subtitle: Massive impression volumes — biggest long-term traffic opportunity

items.title: What is an ERP System? A Beginner's Guide
items.why: Biggest single opportunity. This needs to be a broad educational piece rather than a product page.
keywords: what is an ERP system and how does it work

items.title: Best ERP for Manufacturers
items.why: Manufacturing is E3's core vertical. This should stay definitive and comparison-led.

items.title: Top Benefits of ERP Systems
items.why: Classic top-of-funnel SEO content with room for click growth once rankings improve.

roadmapPhases.label: Phase 3
roadmapPhases.title: Strategic Content — Sells E3
roadmapPhases.subtitle: Lower volume but captures high-intent visitors actively looking for help

items.title: The Value of Investing in a NetSuite Partner
items.why: Directly sells E3's service. Explain what a good partner looks like and why E3 fits.
keywords: why work with a NetSuite partner

items.title: 10 Signs of a Poor NetSuite Implementation
items.why: Problem-aware content. People searching this are frustrated, which makes it a strong lead-in to support services.

items.title: How to Choose the Right ERP Consultant
items.why: Positions E3 as the benchmark for what a good consultant looks like.
keywords: how to choose an ERP consultant

items.title: Why NetSuite Is the Best Choice of Accounting Software

roadmapPhases.label: Phase 4
roadmapPhases.title: Fill the Gaps
roadmapPhases.subtitle: Write over time as earlier content starts ranking

items.title: Understanding the Role of ERP Systems
items.why: Could merge into the broader ERP explainer or stand alone.

items.title: NetSuite Project Management & Financials
items.why: Niche, but relevant for service-led buyers.

items.title: NetSuite Apps & Extensions
items.why: Useful for showing the wider NetSuite ecosystem.

items.title: 7 Warning Signs Your ERP is Holding You Back
items.why: Overlaps with the existing article, so this is more about strengthening and redirecting well.

items.title: Why NetSuite Aftercare is Essential
items.why: Feeds directly into the support proposition.

items.title: Optimise End-of-Year Accounting with NetSuite
items.why: Seasonal topic worth publishing before year-end demand spikes.
